free_text: Neutron Activation Cross Sections for Some Isotopes of
Ruthenium, Palladium, Indium, Tin, and Iridium
Powdered metallic elemental samples were sandwiched
between two iron foils and placed in cylindrical
plastic holders having inner diameter of 2 cm and a
Samples were placed at an angle of 90 deg with respect
to the deuteron beam.  Largest angle subtended by any
sample at the neutron source was +/- 30 deg.
6 cm**2 by 5 mm Ge(Li) detector was used to
follow the photopeaks of the gamma radiation emitted
by the product radioisotopes.  Resolution of the
sections for high yield reactions for each element
were then calculated using this value of neutron flux.
These cross sections were then used as secondary
standards to determine the neutron flux in subsequent
irradiations other isotopes of the same element,
which were made in close geometry for the measurement
Absolute detector efficiency obtained by measuring
photopeak area of gamma rays emitted by 137Cs source
uncertainty in neutron flux and in absolute gamma ray
activities, but not in internal conversion
coefficients or in gamma ray branching.
